channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says a ceasefire extension deal with Iran is near, but core issues remain
item.description: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has not yet decided whether he'll extend a ceasefire with Iran, and Israel continues to attack targets in Lebanon, in spite of a ceasefire there.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Over a thousand Palestinians from Gaza held in Israeli detention without charge
item.description: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.content:encoded: A controversial law allows Israel to hold Palestinians in prison without charge or trial. Israel says it's a necessary for security, but rights groups say it leaves detainees in a legal limbo.
item.dc:creator: Itay Stern

item.title: Boston's Brazilian residents prepare to cheer on their home country in World Cup
item.description: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.content:encoded: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Esteban Bustillos

item.title: Boards of Canada fans remember their hunt for the "Red Moon Party"
item.description: As Boards of Canada release their new album, 'Inferno', diehard fans remember the time when they trekked out into the Scottish countryside trying to find the band's so-called "Red Moon Party."
item.content:encoded: As Boards of Canada release their new album, 'Inferno', diehard fans remember the time when they trekked out into the Scottish countryside trying to find the band's so-called "Red Moon Party."
item.dc:creator: Noah Caldwell

item.title: Celebrating legendary trumpet player Miles Davis' 100th birthday
item.description: This week, the jazz world celebrated what would have been Miles Davis' 100th birthday. The late trumpet player is widely considered one of the most innovative and influential jazz musicians of all time.
item.content:encoded: This week, the jazz world celebrated what would have been Miles Davis' 100th birthday. The late trumpet player is widely considered one of the most innovative and influential jazz musicians of all time.
item.dc:creator: Felix Contreras

item.title: Strategies Americans are using for eating and shopping as food prices skyrocket
item.description: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.content:encoded: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: "I felt guilty for being the chosen one": Ada Ferrer on her memoir about leaving Cuba
item.description: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.content:encoded: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: 20 years since its release, Al Gore talks about the evolution of 'An Inconvenient Truth'
item.description: On the 20th anniversary of the release of his documentary 'An Inconvenient Truth,' Former Vice President Al Gore talks about how his mission to align policy makers across the aisle to work together for climate solutions has evolved.
item.content:encoded: On the 20th anniversary of the release of his documentary 'An Inconvenient Truth,' Former Vice President Al Gore talks about how his mission to align policy makers across the aisle to work together for climate solutions has evolved.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explosion could set back NASA's plans to put astronauts on the moon
item.description: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.content:encoded: Engineers at Jeff Bezos' space company Blue Origin are trying to figure out why their massive New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Rescuers are racing against time to reach 7 men trapped in a cave in Laos
item.description: In Laos, rescue efforts are underway to reach seven men who have been trapped inside a cave for over a week.
item.content:encoded: In Laos, rescue efforts are underway to reach seven men who have been trapped inside a cave for over a week.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby
